Tests variable and function name conflict resolution in JavaScript.

# Purpose
This code demonstrates a simple JavaScript script that defines a variable and a function with the same name, [`foo`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-005foo>). Initially, the variable [`foo`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-005foo>) is set to the value `1`. An assertion follows to check if the variable [`foo`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-005foo>) is equal to `1`. The script then defines a function [`foo`](<#pebble-firmware/src/fw/vendor/jerryscript/tests/jerry-test-suite/13/13-005foo>) that returns the value `1`. This code illustrates the concept of variable and function hoisting in JavaScript, where the function declaration takes precedence over the variable declaration due to hoisting rules.
# Global Variables

---
### foo
- **Type**: ``number``
- **Description**: A global variable `foo` is initialized with the numeric value `1`. The comment `//override` suggests that this variable may override a previous definition or is intended to be overridden.
- **Use**: Used to store the numeric value `1` globally, potentially overriding other definitions of `foo`.
